title: Making Wolfram Tech Available as a Foundation Tool for LLM Systems
summary: Stephen Wolfram introduces CAG (Computation-Augmented Generation) to supplement LLM foundation models with Wolfram's deep computation capabilities and precise knowledge base, enabling accurate mathematical and scientific computations beyond LLM training data.

title: Large-Scale Online Deanonymization with LLMs
summary: Research demonstrating that LLMs can infer personal details (location, occupation, interests) from just a handful of comments and then search to deanonymize users online.

title: The First Fully General Computer Action Model
summary: SI Inc's FDM1 model trained on 11-million-hour video dataset capable of exploring websites, CAD modeling, and real-world driving at 30 FPS—advancing toward general computer control.

title: Learnings from 4 months of Image-Video VAE experiments
summary: Linum团队开源了他们的Image-Video VAE，分享了4个月的实验心得。核心发现：更好的压缩率并不总是与VAE稳定性或下游生成质量成正比，这对视频生成模型的训练具有重要指导意义。

title: Capybara: A Unified Visual Creation Model
summary: An open-source unified visual creation model with ComfyUI integration, capable of image generation and visual content creation. Released under MIT license with active development.

title: Show HN: Moonshine Open-Weights STT models – higher accuracy than WhisperLargev3
summary: New open-source speech-to-text models claiming higher accuracy than Whisper Large V3, with model sizes as small as 26MB for edge deployment. Supports cross-platform integration including Python, iOS, Android, Linux, and embedded devices.

title: Mercury 2: The fastest reasoning LLM, powered by diffusion
summary: Inception Labs launches Mercury 2, the first reasoning diffusion LLM (dLLM) that delivers 5x faster performance than leading speed-optimized autoregressive models while dramatically reducing inference costs.

title: The Missing Semester of Your CS Education – Revised for 2026
summary: MIT's popular practical CS course has been updated for 2026, adding new topics like agentic coding, code quality, packaging/shipping code, and soft skills alongside existing material on version control, debugging, and security.

title: Ladybird adopts Rust
summary: Ladybird browser project announces migration from C++ to Rust as their memory-safe successor language, using AI agents to accelerate the transition after abandoning Swift due to poor C++ interop.

title: Show HN: AI Timeline – 171 LLMs from Transformer (2017) to GPT-5.3 (2026)
summary: Interactive timeline tracking the evolution of major LLMs from the original Transformer architecture through GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, and GPT-5.3, with comprehensive model comparisons.

title: Devirtualization and Static Polymorphism
summary: Deep dive into C++ compiler optimizations, comparing virtual function overhead with CRTP-based static polymorphism. Explains how modern compilers can devirtualize calls even through base pointers.

title: How Will OpenAI Compete?
summary: Benedict Evans analyzes OpenAI's strategic challenges: lacking unique tech, limited user engagement/stickiness, no network effects, and increasing competition from tech incumbents with superior distribution.
